body:
Hi Dad,

Got your voicemail. Yes, I made it to the mat this morning — Tuesday qualifies. Six-thirty, forty minutes, no interruptions until my phone decided a client appeal deadline was more important than savasana. It was not.

I know you've been keeping tabs since January and I'm not complaining. The Tuesday/Thursday/Saturday schedule is holding. Three for three this week so far, which puts me ahead of the February average considerably.

I'll call Sunday. Give Mom my love.

Grace

Grace,

Glad to hear it. Your mother will want confirmation that "made it to the mat" means actual yoga and not just standing near the mat while checking email. I'm choosing to believe you.

Three for three is good. Keep it up through the summer — I know Keystone gets busy and the first thing to go is always the morning routine. Speaking from experience.

Call Sunday before noon if you can. We'll be at the farmers market after that.

Dad

Tom,

Following up on the ticket I submitted Monday regarding Workday access. I still cannot log in to record my hours — the SSO redirect loops back to the Keystone landing page without authenticating. I've cleared cache, tried two browsers, and confirmed my credentials are active through our IT contact, Daniel Porter.

I bill against three active federal matters with deadlines this week, so accurate time entry isn't optional. If this needs to be escalated past tier-one, please do that now rather than after another round of troubleshooting.

Grace Hatfield
Staff Attorney, Keystone Legal Aid
Syracuse, NY

Grace,

Thank you for the detail — that narrows it down quickly. The SSO loop you're describing is a known issue tied to accounts provisioned before our March tenant migration. Your profile wasn't re-linked on the backend.

I've pushed the fix manually on our end. Please try logging in again and let me know if you get through. If the issue persists after 30 minutes, reply here and I'll escalate to our Workday admin team directly so you aren't sitting in another queue.

Apologies for the delay on this.

Tom Hatfield
IT Support, Keystone Legal Aid

Tom,

That did it — I'm in. Time entries for Monday and Tuesday are now logged.

One ask: I have field hockey pickup on Tuesday and Thursday evenings, so my hours on those days are front-loaded in the morning. If Workday flags the irregular punches and generates another ticket, please just note this thread so whoever reviews it has context.

Thanks for the quick fix once it was actually looked at.

Grace

Hi Tom,

Following up on the departure reminder for today's Corcoran practice. The system sent a confirmation ping for Maya Bennett but I never received a response on her end confirming she's set for pickup. Can you check whether the notification actually delivered or got dropped? Last week's reminder disappeared entirely and I only caught it because Lorraine called me.

She needs to be at the Corcoran High School fields by 3:30. If the confirmation is still sitting open on your end, let me know and I'll contact Lorraine Bennett directly rather than wait on the system.

Thanks,
Grace

Hi Grace,

I pulled the logs — Maya Bennett's confirmation request sent at 11:42 AM but shows undelivered. Her notification preference was toggled off after last week's platform update, which explains the pattern you're seeing.

I've re-enabled it and resent the prompt. She should receive it within the next few minutes. I can also apply a flag to her account so that preference can't be overridden by future updates — want me to go ahead with that?

Let me know if she doesn't confirm within the hour and I'll escalate on our end.

Tom
Support Ticket #48821

Tom,

Yes, apply the flag. While you're in there, please add Lorraine Bennett's number as a secondary SMS contact — she's Maya's emergency contact and I coordinate with her regularly through Keystone. She won't miss a text.

I'm in depositions this afternoon and won't be checking email until after 5. If something surfaces before the 3:30 pickup, loop in Daniel Porter — his contact is already on the account.

Grace

Hi Grace,

All done. The account flag is applied, SMS backup is routed to Lorraine Bennett's number, and Daniel Porter is added as an alternate point of contact under your account settings.

The fix is account-wide, so Thursday's reminder will run the same way without any manual check on your end. You're set for today. Good luck in depositions.

Tom
Support Ticket #48821 — Resolved

Tom,

Following up on last week's confusion about the Corcoran fields — wanted to make sure we're squared away before Thursday's practice. The girls have a scrimmage window from 4:15 to 6:00, but I got two different answers from the athletics office about whether the east lot is accessible after 5:30. Can you check with whoever handles facilities over there?

Also: Maya Bennett is confirmed for the carpool. I'm picking her up at 3:45 so we make warm-ups on time. If anything changes on the field side, text me before 2:00 — once I'm out of the office I'm driving and not checking email.

Grace

Grace,

Got it sorted. Called the Corcoran athletics coordinator this morning — east lot stays open until 7:00 on practice days, no exception for scrimmage windows. You're clear through 6:00 with room to spare.

Maya's pickup is on my calendar too, just in case your afternoon runs long at Keystone. Happy to cover the 3:45 run if you need it — just say the word before 1:00. I'll be home all afternoon.

Drive safe Thursday.

Dad

Hi Tom,

Following up on ticket #4821 — the Gmail API OAuth integration for our Keystone Legal Aid account. IT enabled it last Thursday, but I'm still getting 403s when the application attempts to read message threads. The error is consistent across both my account and my colleague Daniel Porter's.

Scopes requested match what was approved: gmail.readonly and gmail.send. The service account has domain-wide delegation enabled per your team's instructions. What I can't verify from my end is whether the admin consent propagation completed on yours.

If you need a screenshot of the error or the full OAuth response body, say the word. I need this resolved before end of week — we have intake filings that depend on automated correspondence logging.

Grace Hatfield
Staff Attorney, Keystone Legal Aid
Syracuse, NY

Hi Grace,

Thanks for the specifics — that's exactly what I needed to start digging. I pulled the audit log on our end and the domain-wide delegation grant did complete, but I'm seeing a client ID mismatch. The ID registered in the Google Workspace admin console doesn't match the one in the service account JSON your application is using.

Can you confirm the service account email address tied to the credentials file? It should end in @[project-id].iam.gserviceaccount.com. Once I have that I can cross-reference against the grant record and correct it if needed. Turnaround on my end is same-day once confirmed.

Best,
Tom Hatfield
Google Workspace Support

Tom,

Service account email is keystone-legal-intake@kla-correspondence.iam.gserviceaccount.com. That's the one in the JSON we're using — Daniel verified it this morning against the file we received from IT.

The delegation was configured under our super admin account, not mine. Our IT lead is out today, but I have read access to the admin console and can pull anything you need — just tell me where to look.

I'm in court until 2 PM. Available after.

Grace

Hi Tom,

Following up on ticket #84312 from last week. The OAuth flow issue we discussed is still blocking me — I'm getting a 403 on the calendar.events.insert call even after regenerating the service account key and re-granting domain-wide delegation. I've verified the scopes include https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar and the impersonated user (me) has edit access on the target calendar.

The practical problem: I'm trying to automate recurring event creation for field hockey pickup reminders on Corcoran High School fields — time-sensitive on Thursday evenings — and right now every call fails silently and I don't catch it until the reminder doesn't fire. That's not a workable setup.

Can you confirm whether the service account needs the G Suite Admin SDK scope in addition, or whether there's a propagation delay I'm not accounting for? Logs attached.

Grace Hatfield
Staff Attorney, Keystone Legal Aid

Hi Grace,

Thanks for the follow-up and the logs — those were helpful. The 403 is almost certainly a delegation issue rather than a scope problem. Domain-wide delegation changes can take up to 24 hours to fully propagate in Google Workspace, but there's a second, faster culprit: the service account's client ID needs to be authorized in Admin Console under Security > API Controls > Domain-wide Delegation. If the client ID listed there doesn't match the one in your current key JSON exactly, you'll get a silent 403 every time regardless of scopes.

Please cross-check the client_id field in your credentials file against what's registered in the Admin Console. If they don't match, update the console entry and allow 15–30 minutes before retesting.

Also confirm: are you calling the API as the service account directly, or impersonating a user? For calendar.events.insert on a user-owned calendar, impersonation is required — direct service account calls won't work.

Let me know what you find.

Tom Hatfield
Google Workspace Support

Tom,

Client IDs matched. Impersonation is configured correctly — I'm using subject: 'grace.hatfield@keystonelegalaid.org' in the JWT claims. Still failing.

I stripped the call down to a minimal repro: single event, hardcoded start/end, no recurrence rules, just a plain insert. Same 403. At this point I want to rule out that my Keystone calendar specifically has a restriction on external API writes — is there a way to verify that from your end, or do I need to loop in our IT admin?

For context, I need this resolved before Thursday. The reminder chain fires at 4:15 p.m. and if I miss the window, my colleague Daniel Porter ends up fielding calls from parents asking whether practice is still on. Not the highest-stakes problem I've had this week, but I'd rather fix it.

Grace

Grace,

That narrows it down considerably. A 403 on a minimal insert with correct impersonation and matching client IDs points to one remaining cause: the calendar itself is set to 'See only free/busy (hide details)' or the sharing settings explicitly block external modifications. Admin-enforced calendar policies in Workspace can override API permissions silently — there's no distinct error code, just a 403.

You'll need your IT admin to check two places in Google Admin Console: (1) Apps > Google Workspace > Calendar > Sharing settings, specifically 'External sharing options for primary calendars,' and (2) whether any resource or organizational policy is applied to your user OU that restricts calendar writes via API.

If your admin confirms no policy is blocking it and the 403 persists, reply with your project number and I'll escalate to a Workspace engineer with backend access.

You have time before Thursday — typical turnaround on escalations is same-day if submitted by noon.

Tom Hatfield
Google Workspace Support

Tom,

Following up on ticket #48821. The Drive API integration is still returning 403s on shared drives when our paralegals attempt batch file uploads — same error as last week. I need this resolved before Friday; we're mid-intake on a housing displacement case and the document workflow is completely blocked.

For context, the service account has the necessary OAuth scopes (drive.file, drive.readonly) applied at the domain level, and the shared drive is confirmed to have the account listed as a Content Manager. The error persists regardless.

Can you confirm whether there's a known issue on your end, or flag this to Tier 2 if not? I don't have bandwidth to keep cycling through the same troubleshooting steps.

Grace Hatfield
Staff Attorney, Keystone Legal Aid

Grace,

Thank you for following up — I apologize the issue has persisted this long.

I reviewed your account and identified the problem: shared drive access requires the supportsAllDrives=true parameter on batch requests, and it looks like your integration is missing that flag. Without it, the API defaults to My Drive behavior and blocks shared drive operations at the permission layer, regardless of the service account's role. This is a common gap when migrating from individual Drive to shared drive setups.

I've attached a corrected request example for your developer. The change is minimal — a single parameter addition — and should resolve the 403s immediately. I've also escalated your ticket to Priority status given the active caseload impact and flagged it for our Drive API specialist to confirm once you've tested.

Please let me know if the fix doesn't hold or if anything else comes up. Happy to schedule a call if that's faster.

Best,
Tom Hatfield
Technical Support, Google Workspace

Tom,

Following up on ticket #4417 I submitted last Thursday regarding the Slack API integration at Keystone Legal Aid. Our intake channel stopped receiving webhook notifications from the client portal — meaning new requests are sitting unacknowledged, which is a problem when we're talking about people in active eviction proceedings.

I tested the endpoint manually using the token your team provided; I'm getting a 200 back but no messages are hitting the channel. Logs attached. Can you confirm whether there's been a configuration change on your end since the 24th?

Need this resolved or a viable workaround by EOD Thursday — we have a staff meeting Friday and I'd rather not explain to Lorraine why the queue went dark for a week.

Grace Hatfield
Staff Attorney, Keystone Legal Aid

Grace,

Thanks for the detail — that log makes the issue pretty clear. The webhook endpoint was automatically rotated on June 25th as part of a platform-wide security update; the old token in your integration config would have silently dropped messages after that without throwing an error. Classic.

To fix it: navigate to your Slack App settings, regenerate the webhook URL under Incoming Webhooks, and swap it into your portal config. The new URL is scoped to the same channel so permissions stay intact. Should take about ten minutes. If you want, I can jump on a quick call tomorrow morning to walk through it — I'm in until noon.

Let me know if the new URL doesn't resolve it and I'll escalate to our integrations team.

Tom Hatfield
Technical Support

Tom,

Swapped the endpoint this morning — intake channel is live again and I've confirmed three test submissions came through clean. Attach the new token to the ticket so whoever inherits this next year doesn't spend a week debugging it.

No call needed, but I appreciate the offer. I'll flag this to Daniel so he knows to watch for the rotation cycle going forward.

Grace

Hi Tom,

Following up on my earlier request to configure the morning yoga check-in reminders. Per our last exchange, the schedule should reflect Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday only — not the full week. I'm still seeing reminders fire on Monday mornings, which isn't accurate and adds noise to the start of a full Keystone workday.

Can you confirm whether the correction was applied on your end? If not, I'd like a timeline for when it will be. I'd rather have this sorted before next Tuesday.

Thanks,
Grace Hatfield

Hi Grace,

I apologize for the continued trouble. I pulled up your account and found the issue — the previous update didn't save correctly and the system reverted to a seven-day schedule. I've corrected it manually: Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday, with your check-in window set at 7:00 AM per your original configuration.

The fix is live now. You should see accurate reminders starting this Thursday. If a Monday check-in fires again, reply directly to this thread and I'll escalate it immediately.

Best,
Tom H.
Customer Support — WellTrack

Tom,

Thursday's reminder came through correctly — and nothing fired Monday morning. Consider that confirmed.

One follow-up item: is it possible to set a different check-in window for Saturdays specifically? My schedule on weekends runs later than weekday mornings, and 7:00 AM conflicts with other commitments. Moving the Saturday window to 8:30 AM while keeping Tuesday and Thursday at 7:00 AM would be the right configuration.

Grace

Hi Grace,

Glad to hear Thursday landed on schedule.

I've updated your Saturday check-in window to 8:30 AM — the change is live now. Tuesday and Thursday remain at 7:00 AM. The new time will take effect this coming Saturday.

For future adjustments, you can update individual day windows directly under Account Settings → Reminder Preferences, or reach back out and I'll handle it. Hope the rest of your week runs smoothly.

Best,
Tom H.
Customer Support — WellTrack

Tom,

Following up on ticket #4417. It's been three business days since I reported the issue with our workday start configuration in the case management portal — client intake queues aren't populating until 9:47 a.m. instead of 8:00, which means attorneys are walking in to dead screens during our highest-volume intake window.

Keystone sees the bulk of same-day walk-ins between 8 and 10. That 107-minute lag isn't a minor inconvenience — it's clients who can't wait being turned away. I need a confirmed resolution timeline, not another automated acknowledgment.

If there's a backend sync issue or a timezone misconfiguration, I can get our IT contact Daniel Porter on a call this afternoon. Just say the word.

Grace Hatfield
Staff Attorney, Keystone Legal Aid
Syracuse, NY

Grace,

Apologies for the delay — I should have followed up sooner and I appreciate you being direct about the impact.

I've identified the issue: a daylight saving time offset was applied incorrectly during last week's server migration, pushing your tenant's workday start trigger to UTC-adjusted 9:47 rather than the configured 8:00 Eastern. I've pushed a corrected configuration to your instance and confirmed the intake queue is now initializing at 8:00 a.m. as of this morning.

No call with Mr. Porter should be necessary, but I've attached a brief technical summary in case he wants it for your records. If the queue behaves correctly through tomorrow morning's start, I'll close the ticket. If anything looks off, reply here and I'll escalate to our senior infrastructure team immediately — no waiting.

Thank you for flagging this clearly. It made the diagnosis straightforward.

Best,
Tom Hatfield
Client Support, Platform Operations

Tom,

Following up on the departure reminder for this afternoon's practice at Corcoran. The reminder fired but I didn't see a confirmation that Maya's actually set. I'm wrapping a brief at 3:30 and leaving from Keystone around 3:45 — if she needs a pickup from the Eastside I can make it work, but I need to know before I'm already on 81 heading south.

Confirm either way.

Grace

Grace,

Apologies for the delay — Maya confirmed at 10:47 this morning. She has her own ride to Corcoran today, gear included. You're clear to go direct.

Also flagging: I have a note that next Tuesday is a yoga morning for you, which puts your Keystone start later than usual. If practice schedules shift this week, I wanted you to have the heads-up before it stacked. I'll update the reminder block if anything changes.

Tom

Tom,

Good. Direct to Corcoran it is.

On Tuesday — yes, I'm aware yoga exists on my own calendar, but I appreciate the flag. If practice moves and somehow conflicts with a 6 a.m. yoga block, that will be the least of my scheduling problems.

Update the reminder if Coach Ellison actually commits to a time. She's been 'tentatively' shifting things for three weeks running, so hold the change until it's confirmed.

Grace

Tom,

Following up on last week's exchange regarding Tuesday and Thursday practice slots at Corcoran's south fields. I haven't received confirmation that the permit amendment went through for the 5:30 PM start time. I need that locked in before I can set Maya Bennett's carpool schedule — her mother Lorraine has been waiting on me for the week's logistics and I'd rather not hand her another moving target.

Can you confirm the amended permit is on file and whether the lights are covered through 7:00 PM? That's the detail that keeps slipping.

Grace Hatfield
Staff Attorney, Keystone Legal Aid
Syracuse, NY

Grace,

Apologies for the delay on this — the parks department kicked the permit back for a minor correction on the field designation number. I resubmitted Monday and received confirmation this morning. The amended permit is now on file for Tuesdays and Thursdays, 5:30–7:00 PM, south fields, lights included through end of session.

I'm attaching the confirmation PDF. You should be good to finalize the carpool schedule. Let me know if anything looks off on the dates — the October 14th slot flagged a potential conflict with a school event but facilities says it's resolved.

Tom Hatfield
Athletic Facilities Coordinator, Corcoran High School

Tom,

Got the PDF — thank you. October 14th works; I checked the school calendar and the conflict was a scheduling ghost from the old system.

One remaining item: I need written confirmation that the equipment shed on the south end will be unlocked for practice days. The coach has flagged it twice. If that requires a separate form I'll fill it out today — just point me to it. I'm in court Thursday morning but reachable by email before noon.

Grace

Grace,

No separate form needed. I've added a standing unlock order for the south equipment shed, Tuesdays and Thursdays, effective this week through the end of the fall season. Coach Merritt has been notified directly.

You're all set. If anything else comes up mid-season, email works best — I check it twice daily. Good luck to the team.

Tom Hatfield
Athletic Facilities Coordinator, Corcoran High School
